title: Hook integration for test-planning lifecycle (UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse/SessionEnd)
description:
Purpose
Implement hook-level lifecycle integration for the test-planning skill so testing strategy is consistently introduced at planning time, validated at closure time, and reviewed at session end.

Dependency Requirement
This epic depends on completion of:
- xtrm-thn4 (Test-planning v1.1)
The hook automation in this epic must consume and enforce the improved skill guidance from that foundation epic.

Problem Statement
Today, test-planning quality relies heavily on manual invocation and agent discipline. Even with good skill docs, drift happens during high-throughput issue planning and closure. We need deterministic hook touchpoints that provide high-signal nudges (and optional strict gating) without noisy false positives.

Goals
1) Integrate test-planning at precise lifecycle points with minimal noise
2) Detect planning and closure intents automatically from tool events
3) Run a closure safety net to prevent implementation issues from being closed without planned test coverage
4) Preserve configurability through policy modes (advisory vs strict)
5) Maintain parity between Claude hook behavior and Pi extension behavior

Non-Goals
- Do not rewrite the core test-planning strategy content in this epic (owned by dependency epic)
- Do not enforce universal strict blocking by default
- Do not introduce heavyweight background indexing/polling

Scope of Work
A. UserPromptSubmit Intent Detection
- Add prompt-level detection for planning/closure phrases:
  - “create issues”, “break into tasks”, “close issue”, “bd create”, “bd close”, “bd children”
- On detection, emit targeted suggestion to apply test-planning skill
- Ensure suppression for clearly unrelated prompts to avoid noise

B. PostToolUse Deterministic Triggering (Bash + bd commands)
- For tool executions containing bd commands:
  1. On `bd create`:
     - classify issue as implementation vs non-implementation
     - check for matching/batched companion test issue
     - if absent: emit recommendation (advisory mode) or enforce next-step gate (strict mode)
  2. On `bd close`:
     - run closure gate check (existing test issue? still accurate to built scope?)
     - if missing/outdated: emit actionable update/create guidance
- Include parsing resilience for flag order variations and quoted values

C. Stop / SessionEnd Safety Net
- At session end, summarize implementation issues touched without test-plan linkage
- Default behavior: non-blocking warning + suggested bd commands
- Strict mode option: require acknowledgement or block stop until recorded exception path

D. Policy Modes + Configuration
- Add explicit config modes:
  - `advisory` (default): suggest, never block
  - `strict-close`: block/deny close path when no test plan linkage exists
  - `strict-session`: enforce end-session acknowledgement
- Document env/config toggles and precedence order

E. Claude/Pi Parity Layer
- Ensure equivalent behavior mapping in:
  - Claude hooks configuration/path
  - Pi extension equivalents
- Add parity matrix documenting event, trigger, action, and strictness behavior per runtime

F. Messaging UX
- Standardize concise, actionable messages:
  - what failed/missing
  - why it matters
  - exact `bd` command snippets to remediate
- Avoid verbose diagnostics in normal path; detailed logs only in debug mode

G. Observability + Debugging
- Add debug mode to log per-event decisions:
  - invoked / skipped / matched-intent / remediation-generated
- Include skip reasons for false-positive tuning

Deliverables
1) Hook/extension integration implementation for 3 lifecycle points:
- UserPromptSubmit
- PostToolUse (bd create/bd close)
- Stop/SessionEnd

2) Runtime configuration model for advisory/strict policies

3) Parity documentation (Claude vs Pi behavior table)

4) Test coverage for trigger detection and gating behavior

Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: Planning-intent prompts trigger test-planning suggestion with low false positives
- AC2: `bd create` path detects implementation issues and checks companion test-plan presence
- AC3: `bd close` path runs closure gate checks and emits deterministic remediation
- AC4: Session end summarizes unresolved test-plan gaps; strict mode behavior works as configured
- AC5: Advisory and strict modes are configurable, documented, and covered by tests
- AC6: Claude and Pi implementations show behavior parity for equivalent events
- AC7: Messaging includes copy/paste-safe bd remediation commands

Risk Analysis
- Risk: Over-triggering could create friction
  - Mitigation: conservative intent matching + skip reasons + advisory default
- Risk: Command parsing edge cases
  - Mitigation: robust parser tests for quoting/flag order/compound commands
- Risk: Runtime drift between Claude and Pi
  - Mitigation: parity test suite + parity matrix as release gate

Suggested Work Breakdown
1. Implement intent detector + unit tests
2. Implement PostToolUse bd parser + create/close handlers
3. Implement SessionEnd safety net
4. Add mode config and wire toggles
5. Build parity tests (Claude vs Pi)
6. Add docs and operational examples

Dependencies
- Blocked by: xtrm-thn4

Definition of Complete
This epic is complete when test-planning is automatically and predictably surfaced across planning, closure, and session-end lifecycle events—with configurable strictness and verified Claude/Pi parity—built on top of the upgraded v1.1 skill content.
close_reason: Closed during board cleanup: wdnd=ralph-loop already exists; cnsd=dolt push not configured; 25v8=test-planning invoked on demand, hook automation not needed

title: Test-planning v1.1: deterministic strategy, anti-pattern controls, and DoD templates
description:
Purpose
Elevate the existing skills/test-planning skill from a strong baseline to a production-grade planning standard for test strategy in bd-driven workflows.

Background
The current test-planning skill already provides excellent foundations:
- layer-based classification (core/boundary/shell)
- strategy routing (TDD/spec-first/property/contract/characterization/example-based)
- planning-phase + closure-gate behavior
- batching guidance and bd-native issue structures

This epic upgrades that baseline with sharper quality controls, deterministic prioritization, and explicit Definition-of-Done standards so generated/updated test issues are consistently actionable.

Problem Statement
Current skill guidance can still produce variability across agents/sessions:
- anti-patterns are described indirectly but not enforced as a standard checklist
- test issue priorities are not consistently normalized against implementation risk
- layer guidance exists, but completion criteria are not codified as strict DoD templates
- teams may over-focus on broad coverage percentages over critical-path confidence
- advisory vs enforcement boundaries are implied but not explicit

Goals
1) Add explicit anti-pattern prevention checklist for planning + closure review
2) Add risk-based priority model for generated test issues
3) Add per-layer Definition of Done templates with concrete pass criteria
4) Replace blanket coverage framing with critical-path and risk-path expectations
5) Clarify policy boundary: what skill recommends vs what hooks enforce

Non-Goals
- Do not implement hook automation in this epic (tracked separately)
- Do not add new runtime dependencies
- Do not rewrite quality-gates behavior itself beyond documentation alignment

Scope of Work
A. Anti-Pattern Checklist Integration
- Add a standardized checklist section used at both triggers (planning and closure)
- Required checks:
  1. assertion-free tests
  2. tautological assertions (self-fulfilling logic)
  3. context leakage/shared mutable state
  4. over-mocking internal collaborators
  5. test cases that cannot fail under realistic regressions
- Add “how to detect” and “how to fix” bullets per anti-pattern

B. Priority Heuristics for Test Issues
- Introduce deterministic priority mapping:
  - P0/P1: boundary/security/auth/protocol compatibility/regression-critical paths
  - P1/P2: core domain logic with high business impact
  - P2/P3: low-risk internals, non-critical adapters
  - P3/P4: polish-level test debt
- Add inheritance rule from implementation issue priority with bounded adjustment
- Add examples for when tests should be equal priority vs +1 priority

C. Layer-Specific Definition of Done Templates
- Core DoD template:
  - positive + negative path assertions
  - edge cases explicitly enumerated
  - invariants/properties included where applicable
  - deterministic fixtures and no shared mutable state
- Boundary DoD template:
  - schema/contract assertions
  - error code and retry/fallback behavior where relevant
  - drift-safe assertions (field presence/types, not brittle internals)
  - live-first policy and fallback hierarchy documented
- Shell DoD template:
  - end-to-end observable outcomes
  - failure-mode UX (exit codes/messages) assertions
  - cross-component wiring checks

D. Coverage Guidance Modernization
- Remove/avoid blanket numeric coverage framing as primary success metric
- Require “critical path map” in issue description:
  - paths covered
  - risk rationale
  - known deferred paths (with follow-up issue references)

E. Enforcement Boundary Clarification
- Add explicit section in SKILL.md:
  - advisory from skill (planning quality, strategy selection)
  - enforced by hooks/extensions (quality-gates, branch/claim rules)
- Include examples to avoid policy confusion

Deliverables
1) Updated skill file:
- skills/test-planning/SKILL.md with all new sections/templates/checklists

2) Example patterns in-document:
- at least 2 end-to-end issue description examples using new templates

3) Consistency alignment:
- terminology alignment with using-quality-gates and broader xtrm testing philosophy

Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: SKILL.md includes a mandatory anti-pattern checklist used in both trigger flows
- AC2: Priority heuristic table exists with deterministic mapping + examples
- AC3: DoD templates exist for core, boundary, shell with concrete pass/fail criteria
- AC4: Guidance uses critical-path coverage framing, not blanket percentage mandates
- AC5: Advisory vs enforcement boundaries are explicitly documented with examples
- AC6: At least two realistic issue examples show the new format end-to-end

Risks
- Over-prescriptive templates could increase issue creation overhead
- Priority heuristics may need calibration after first few real-world uses

Mitigations
- Keep templates concise and structured
- Collect first-iteration feedback and tune thresholds in follow-up task(s)

Dependencies
- None (foundation epic)

Out of Scope Follow-ups
- Hook-based automation and gating integration (separate epic)

Definition of Complete
This epic is complete when the test-planning skill documentation is upgraded to a deterministic, reusable standard that reliably produces high-quality, risk-aware test planning issues without requiring runtime hook automation.
close_reason: All 5 sections added to skills/test-planning/SKILL.md: anti-pattern checklist, priority heuristics, per-layer DoD templates, critical-path coverage guidance, advisory-vs-enforcement boundary. Two v1.1 format examples included.

title: Test: policy compiler — integration + schema validation tests
description:
## What this covers

Covers: xtrm-ei6 (policy schema), xtrm-o52 (compiler), xtrm-cuk (main-guard + beads policies)

## Layer classification

- `policies/schema.json` → Core (pure data, JSON Schema definition)
- `scripts/compile-policies.mjs` → Shell (CLI tool: reads files, writes hooks.json)

## Strategy: Integration tests (shell layer, deterministic I/O)

The compiler is a pure function over the filesystem: given N policy files → produces hooks.json. No external I/O beyond file reads/writes. Integration tests with fixture directories are ideal.

## Test file structure

`tests/compile-policies/` (or `scripts/tests/` if colocated with the script):
- `fixtures/policies/` — sample policy JSON files
- `fixtures/expected-hooks.json` — expected compiler output
- `compile-policies.test.mjs` — test suite

## What to assert

### Compiler correctness
- [ ] Given the 5 current policy files → output matches `hooks/hooks.json` exactly (golden file test)
- [ ] Same-matcher hooks from multiple policies merge into one group (order preserved)
- [ ] `order` field controls policy execution sequence within the same event
- [ ] Policies with `runtime: pi` are excluded from hooks.json output
- [ ] No matcher entry is emitted as `null` or empty string — omitted entirely

### CLI flags
- [ ] `--dry-run`: prints JSON to stdout, does NOT write hooks.json
- [ ] `--check`: exits 0 when hooks.json matches; exits 1 when stale (with useful error message)
- [ ] Write mode: overwrites hooks.json, prints per-policy hook counts

### Schema validation (if validation is added to compiler)
- [ ] Policy missing required `id` → validation error
- [ ] Policy with unknown field → validation error (additionalProperties: false)
- [ ] Policy with invalid `runtime` value → validation error

### Edge cases
- [ ] Empty policies/ directory → empty hooks object `{}`
- [ ] Policy with no `claude.hooks` → contributes nothing to output
- [ ] Timeout omitted → no timeout field in generated entry

## Acceptance criteria

All assertions pass. `npm run check-policies` passes in CI (add to .github/workflows/ci.yml).
close_reason: 12 integration tests written in cli/test/compile-policies.test.ts — covers golden file, --dry-run, --check, write mode, macro expansion, pi exclusion, no null matchers, timeout field, SessionStart merging. All 306 tests pass.

title: Add Pi extension equivalent of beads-memory-gate
description:
## Description

Port the beads-memory-gate stop hook to a Pi extension. The Claude-side hook fires at session end and prompts the agent to persist insights via `bd remember` when the session's claimed issue was closed.

The Pi equivalent should:
- Hook into Pi's session-end / stop event
- Check `getSessionClaim` (or equivalent bd kv lookup) for the session claim
- If the claim is now closed, prompt the agent to evaluate insights worth persisting
- Accept a marker signal (bd kv or equivalent) to clear the gate once evaluation is done

## Reference

Claude implementation: `hooks/beads-memory-gate.mjs` (recently fixed in #98 to scope to session claim instead of all-time closed issues).
close_reason: Closed

title: memory-gate fires every session + marker file unwritable on main
description:
## Problem

beads-memory-gate stop hook fires every session because it checks `bd list --status=closed` (all ever-closed issues) rather than issues closed *this* session. Once triggered, it requires `touch .beads/.memory-gate-done` — but the absolute path was being used, which doesn't match the main-guard allowlist pattern `/^touch\s+\.beads\//`. Using relative path `touch .beads/.memory-gate-done` from project CWD works fine.

## Root Cause

**beads-memory-gate.mjs**: checked all-time closed issues instead of scoping to the current session's claimed issue.

## Fix

Only fire the gate if the session had a claimed issue that is now closed. Use `getSessionClaim(sessionId, cwd)` to retrieve the claim, then check if it appears in `bd list --status=closed`.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate xtrm-tools to Claude Code plugin structure
description:
Restructure xtrm-tools as a first-class Claude Code plugin.

Current state: xtrm CLI installs hooks to ~/.claude/hooks/, wires settings.json, copies skills to ~/.agents/skills/. This causes permanent drift via PROTECTED_KEYS — requires install/sync/clean/prune machinery.

Plugin approach eliminates all of that:
- hooks/hooks.json with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} portable paths — no wiring, no drift
- skills/ auto-discovered by Claude Code — no copying needed
- .mcp.json for MCP server registration
- Pi extensions remain separate (config/pi/extensions/ → Pi's own install)
- xtrm CLI shrinks to: project init, gitnexus setup, bd init

This is the structural prerequisite for the policy unification epic (k9bg).
The plugin hooks/hooks.json becomes the target output for the policy compiler (o52).

Deliverables:
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest
- hooks/hooks.json using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}
- skills/ mirrored from current skills/
- .mcp.json for core MCP servers
- Retire safeMergeConfig / PROTECTED_KEYS / install-sync hook machinery
- Update xtrm CLI: install plugin, not manage hooks directly
close_reason: Scaffolding phase complete: .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT paths), .mcp.json (gitnexus added). CLI retire deferred to follow-on.

title: Policy unification + precise main-guard command-intent enforcement
description:
Epic: unified enforcement model where policies are defined once and compiled to both Claude Code plugin hooks and Pi extensions.

Prerequisite: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration — must complete first)

Child issues (execute in order):
1. xtrm-ei6 — define policy schema
2. xtrm-o52 — build policy compiler (input: schema → output: hooks.json + Pi .ts)
3. xtrm-cuk — migrate main-guard + beads policies
4. xtrm-76v — migrate service-skills + quality-gates policies
5. xtrm-79m — cross-runtime parity test suite

End state: no hand-written hooks or Pi extension duplication. All enforcement derives from policies/*.yaml. The plugin structure (8r52) is the delivery mechanism for Claude-side compiled output.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add cross-runtime parity test suite for policy enforcement
description:
Test suite that verifies a given policy produces equivalent enforcement behavior across both Claude (plugin hooks) and Pi (extensions).

Depends on: xtrm-o52 (policy compiler — need compiled artifacts to test)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

For each compiled policy:
- Claude side: simulate PreToolUse/PostToolUse events, assert block/allow/augment behavior
- Pi side: simulate Pi event handlers, assert same outcome
- Diff report: flag any behavioral divergence between runtimes

Test harness should run in CI (no live agent required). Mock event payloads based on policy schema from ei6.
close_reason: Closed

title: Build policy compiler to generate Claude hooks and Pi mappings
description:
Build a compiler that takes canonical policy definitions (ei6 schema) and generates both Claude and Pi enforcement artifacts.

Depends on: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration), xtrm-ei6 (policy schema)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

Input: policies/*.yaml (canonical policy definitions)
Output 1: plugin/hooks/hooks.json — Claude Code plugin hook wiring (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} paths)
Output 2: config/pi/extensions/<policy>.ts — Pi extension TypeScript stubs

The plugin structure (8r52) defines where Output 1 lives. Without it, there is no clean delivery target.
The policy schema (ei6) defines what the input looks like.

Compiler should:
- Validate policies against schema
- Emit hooks.json entries with correct matchers, timeouts, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} references
- Emit Pi extension event handlers with matching logic
- Report gaps where a policy cannot be expressed in one or both runtimes
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify hook/extension policy model across Claude and Pi
description:
Define a single canonical policy schema that drives enforcement on both Claude Code (hooks) and Pi (extensions).

Context: xtrm-tools is migrating to a Claude Code plugin structure (xtrm-8r52). The plugin's hooks/hooks.json is the Claude-side delivery target. Pi extensions remain in config/pi/extensions/. A unified policy model means one definition generates both.

Depends on: xtrm-8r52 (plugin migration — establishes the target structure)
Part of epic: xtrm-k9bg

Policy schema should capture:
- event (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, etc.)
- matcher (tool pattern)
- action (block | notify | augment)
- condition (optional predicate)
- runtime targets (claude | pi | both)

Output consumed by o52 (policy compiler) to generate hooks/hooks.json and Pi extension TypeScript.
close_reason: Closed

title: Eliminate tdd-guard completely — remove from Claude hooks, Pi extension, project-skills, and skills catalog
description:
Per TDD research brief (2026-03-16) and global-first architecture (4xr6): tdd-guard is eliminated entirely.

Research findings:
- TDD gates provide 9-30% improvement ONLY when tests are provided as EXTERNAL specification
- Same agent writing both tests + impl = self-consistency theater
- LLM tests are fragile (40% coverage, 34-62% invalid syntax)
- PostToolUse quality gates are the correct universal default

Elimination scope:
1. hooks/tdd-guard.mjs (or equivalent) — delete
2. policies/tdd-guard.json — delete
3. project-skills/tdd-guard/ — delete entire directory
4. skills/using-TDD/ — keep skill (TDD as optional opt-in guidance), remove any 'guard' references
5. Pi extension config/pi/extensions/tdd-guard.ts (if exists) — delete
6. hooks.json — recompile after policy deletion
7. XTRM-GUIDE.md — remove from project skills table
8. using-xtrm skill — remove references to tdd-guard

Part of epic 4xr6 (global-first architecture). 4xr6.1 scope updated to exclude tdd-guard.
close_reason: tdd-guard fully eliminated: project-skills/tdd-guard/ deleted, .claude/hooks/tdd-guard-pretool-bridge.cjs deleted, all references removed from XTRM-GUIDE.md and using-xtrm SKILL.md

title: Consolidate duplicated hook pruning logic
description:
Two different implementations of hook pruning exist:

- atomic-config.ts: pruneStaleWrappers()
- install-project.ts: pruneStaleHooks()

Both have slightly different logic for detecting stale hooks.

**Impact**: Inconsistent behavior, maintenance burden, potential bugs from divergence.

**Recommendation**: Consolidate into a single implementation in atomic-config.ts.
close_reason: Stale: CLI Bugfixes Audit from 2026-03-17 no longer relevant

title: Use manifest for drift detection
description:
The .jaggers-sync-manifest.json tracks:

```json
{
  "lastSync": "timestamp",
  "repoRoot": "...",
  "items": 42
}
```

But it doesn't track individual file hashes. The diff.ts recalculates hashes every time instead of using the manifest for comparison.

**Impact**: Unnecessary file I/O on every status check, inaccurate drift detection.

**Recommendation**: Store file hashes in manifest for accurate drift detection.
close_reason: Done as part of 9bl fix — manifest stores per-file hashes, compareItem uses them

title: Implement cross-target transaction rollback
description:
Currently, if sync fails on the 3rd target after successfully syncing 2 targets:

- No automatic rollback across targets
- Each target has its own backup system but they're not coordinated
- User ends up with partially synced environment

**Impact**: Users can be left in an inconsistent state after partial sync failures.

**Recommendation**: Implement cross-target transaction with coordinated rollback.

title: Debug: Beads claim workflow not working reliably
description:
## Problem

The beads claim workflow should auto-set `bd kv claimed:<session_id> <issue_id>` when running `bd update <id> --claim`, but it's not working reliably.

## Expected Behavior

1. Run `bd update <issue-id> --claim`
2. Hook or extension auto-sets `bd kv set "claimed:<session_id>" "<issue-id>"`
3. Statusline shows claimed issue (e.g., `bd:r34◐`)
4. Edits are unblocked

## Actual Behavior

- The claim is set on the issue (status becomes `in_progress`)
- But the kv entry is NOT set
- Statusline shows open count (`bd:2○`) instead of claimed issue
- Manual `bd kv set` is required

---

## Architecture

### Components Involved

| Component | Location | Role |
|-----------|----------|------|
| Pi Extension | `cli/extensions/beads.ts` | Should catch `bd update --claim` via `tool_result` event and set kv |
| Pi Extension | `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` | Reads kv to display beads chip in statusline |
| PostToolUse Hook | `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` | Should catch `bd update --claim` and set kv |
| PreToolUse Hook | `hooks/beads-edit-gate.mjs` | Checks kv to allow/block edits |

### Session ID Strategy (Attempted)

| Attempt | Session Key Source | Problem |
|---------|-------------------|---------|
| 1. PID | `process.pid.toString()` | Hook runs in subprocess, different PID than Pi |
| 2. Project path | `input.cwd` | Works but doesn't match Pi's PID-based key |
| 3. session_id from hook input | `input.session_id` | Should be UUID from Pi's `sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| 4. ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId() | Pi extension API | Should return UUID matching hook's `input.session_id` |

---

## Everything Tried

### Attempt 1: Pi Extension tool_result Handler

**File:** `cli/extensions/beads.ts`

```typescript
pi.on("tool_result", async (event, ctx) => {
  if (isBashToolResult(event)) {
    const command = event.input.command;
    if (command && /\bbd\s+update\b/.test(command) && /--claim\b/.test(command)) {
      const issueMatch = command.match(/\bbd\s+update\s+(\S+)/);
      if (issueMatch) {
        const issueId = issueMatch[1];
        const cwd = getCwd(ctx);
        const sessionId = process.pid.toString(); // Problem: different from hook
        await SubprocessRunner.run("bd", ["kv", "set", `claimed:${sessionId}`, issueId], { cwd });
        // ...
      }
    }
  }
});
```

**Result:** Extension did not appear to fire on `bd update --claim`. No `✅ **Beads**` message appeared.

**Suspected Issue:** Extension events may require `/reload` or session restart to take effect.

---

### Attempt 2: PostToolUse Hook

**File:** `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs`

```javascript
import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';

function main() {
  const input = JSON.parse(readFileSync(0, 'utf-8'));
  if (input.tool_name !== 'Bash') process.exit(0);
  
  const command = input.tool_input?.command || '';
  if (!/\bbd\s+update\b/.test(command) || !/--claim\b/.test(command)) {
    process.exit(0);
  }

  const match = command.match(/\bbd\s+update\s+(\S+)/);
  const issueId = match[1];
  const sessionId = input.session_id; // Should be UUID

  spawnSync('bd', ['kv', 'set', `claimed:${sessionId}`, issueId], { ... });
}
```

**Result:** Hook never fired. Debug log file was never created.

**Suspected Issue:** 
- Hooks may only be loaded at session start, not via `/reload`
- The settings.json had a malformed entry that was fixed, but session needs restart

---

### Attempt 3: Manual Session Key Coordination

Tried multiple session key strategies:

1. **PID** - Hook PID `1225881` != Pi PID `1107931`
2. **Project path** - Works but inconsistent with statusline
3. **session_id from hook input** - Should work but hook not firing
4. **ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()** - Should return UUID

**Files updated to use UUID:**
- `cli/extensions/beads.ts` → `ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.()`
- `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` → `ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.()`
- `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` → `input.session_id`

---

### Attempt 4: Reinstall Hooks

```bash
mv ~/.claude/settings.json ~/.claude/settings.json.broken
# Created clean settings.json
node cli/dist/index.cjs install all -y
```

**Result:** Hooks reinstalled correctly, but still not firing.

---

### Attempt 5: Add Debug Logging to Hook

```javascript
appendFileSync('/tmp/beads-hook.log', `${new Date().toISOString()} invoked\n`);
```

**Result:** Log file never created. Hook not invoked at all.

---

### Attempt 6: Test Hook Manually

```bash
echo '{"hook_event_name":"PostToolUse","tool_name":"Bash",...}' | node ~/.claude/hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs
```

**Result:** Hook works correctly when invoked directly. Outputs expected JSON.

---

### Attempt 7: Check settings.json Configuration

```json
"PostToolUse": [
  {
    "hooks": [
      {
        "type": "command",
        "command": "node \"/home/dawid/.claude/hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs\"",
        "timeout": 5000
      }
    ],
    "matcher": "Bash"
  },
  // ... other hooks
]
```

**Result:** Configuration looks correct.

---

## Current State

| Component | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `beads.ts` extension | Updated | Uses `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| `custom-footer.ts` extension | Updated | Uses `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()` |
| `beads-claim-sync.mjs` hook | Updated | Uses `input.session_id` |
| `~/.claude/settings.json` | Regenerated | Clean, hooks wired correctly |
| Hook invocation | NOT FIRING | Log never created, hook never called |
| Extension tool_result | UNCLEAR | May or may not be firing |

---

## Questions to Answer

1. **Why is the PostToolUse hook not firing?**
   - Is there a session cache that needs clearing?
   - Does `/reload` reload hooks or only extensions/skills?
   - Is there a hook loading log we can check?

2. **Is the Pi extension tool_result event firing?**
   - How to verify?
   - Does `/reload` reload extensions?

3. **What is the correct session ID source?**
   - Hook input provides `session_id` field
   - Pi provides `ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId()`
   - Are these the same UUID?

---

## Files to Review

- `cli/extensions/beads.ts` - Pi extension for claim handling
- `config/pi/extensions/custom-footer.ts` - Statusline display
- `hooks/beads-claim-sync.mjs` - PostToolUse hook
- `hooks/beads-edit-gate.mjs` - PreToolUse edit blocker
- `config/hooks.json` - Canonical hook configuration
- `~/.claude/settings.json` - Runtime hook wiring

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a fresh Pi session
2. Run `bd update <issue-id> --claim`
3. Check `bd kv list | grep claimed`
4. Expected: New entry with session UUID
5. Actual: No new entry

---

## Workaround

Manually set the claim:
```bash
# Get your session ID from statusline or hook error message
bd kv set "claimed:<session-id>" "<issue-id>"
```

---

## Related Issues

- xtrm-xoi (session key coordination - may be obsolete after this investigation)
- PR #76 (beads-claim-sync hook - merged but not working)
close_reason: Fixed and released in PR #78 / npm 2.1.30

title: Bug: git reset --hard origin/main blocked on protected branch — breaks post-merge sync step
description: After gh pr merge --squash, the agent must run git checkout main && git reset --hard origin/main to sync local main with remote. The reset --hard step is not in main-guard.mjs SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS, so once xtrm-3k7 is fixed (Bash added to matcher), this required workflow step will be blocked by the default-deny. Fix: add a pattern for git reset --hard origin/<protected-branch> to SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS in main-guard.mjs, scoped dynamically to the protected branches list. Also standardize sync guidance: post-push hook says git pull --ff-only but main-guard workflow message says git reset --hard origin/master — pick one (reset --hard is correct after squash merge). deps: xtrm-3k7

title: Bug: main-guard.mjs Bash handler never fires — Bash missing from hooks.json matcher

But in config/hooks.json the hook is only registered with matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit|mcp__serena__*. Bash is absent from the matcher, so the hook never receives Bash tool events. The entire Bash-protection code path is dead. Fix: add Bash to the matcher for main-guard.mjs in config/hooks.json (alongside the existing Write|Edit|... matcher, or as a separate entry). Verification: on main branch, git commit should be blocked with the feature-branch workflow message.

title: service-skills-set: git hooks not executed when core.hooksPath is .beads/hooks
description:
xtrm install project service-skills-set installs pre-commit and pre-push into .githooks/ and reports '✓ activated in .git/hooks/'. However, when git core.hooksPath is already set to .beads/hooks (set by beads), git never executes .githooks/. The .beads/hooks/pre-commit and pre-push only run 'bd hooks run pre-commit/pre-push' and do not chain to .githooks/.

Repro: after install, run 'git config core.hooksPath' → returns '.beads/hooks'. Run 'git commit' → .githooks/pre-commit never fires.

Fix options:
  A) Detect core.hooksPath at install time and install into that directory instead
  B) Append a chain call to .githooks scripts into the existing .beads/hooks/pre-commit and pre-push
  C) Register the service-skills git hook scripts with beads via 'bd hooks add'
close_reason: Fixed in 9af7948: service installer now chains .githooks into active core.hooksPath (including .beads/hooks)

title: py/ts quality-gate hooks silently skip Serena edits (relative_path not extracted)
description:
Both quality-check.py and quality-check.cjs extract the edited file path from tool_input.file_path or tool_input.path. Serena tools (replace_symbol_body, insert_after_symbol, insert_before_symbol, rename_symbol) pass relative_path instead. Result: the hooks fire (matcher is correct) but immediately exit 0 with 'No file path found' — no quality check runs at all.

Repro:
  echo '{"tool_name":"mcp__serena__replace_symbol_body","tool_input":{"relative_path":"src/core/store.ts","name_path":"foo","body":"x"}}' | python3 .claude/hooks/quality-check.py
  # exits 0 with 'No file to check'

Fix needed in:
  - quality-check.py :: extract_file_path() — add: tool_input.get('relative_path')
  - quality-check.cjs :: equivalent file path extraction logic

When relative_path is present, resolve it against CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR to get the absolute path before running checks.
close_reason: Fixed in 9af7948: py/ts quality hooks now resolve Serena relative_path

close_reason: Replaced wildcard Serena matcher with explicit full tool matcher list in global + project hook configs; added tdd-guard pretool bridge to skip non-code edits and pass through code edits to tdd-guard.

title: Tighten main-guard allowlist: block mutating git checkout forms

Follow-up to main-guard v2: SAFE_BASH_PATTERNS currently allow any git checkout/switch command on protected branches. This permits mutating forms like 'git checkout -- <path>' and 'git checkout <rev> -- <path>'.

Added regression tests for git checkout -- <path>, git checkout HEAD -- <path>, and git switch --detach HEAD. Validation: npm test --prefix cli -- test/hooks.test.ts (17/17 pass).

Regression after PR #18: hooks/beads-gate-utils.mjs getTotalWork uses repeated --status flags that can miss open issues, causing beads-edit-gate fail-open when no in_progress exists. Also CI does not execute new cli/test/hooks.test.ts because workflow only runs root npm test placeholder.

Validation: npm test --prefix cli -- test/hooks.test.ts (12/12 pass), manual hook repro now exits 2 with no claim and open work.

Migration in PR #17 copied old specialists-bundled hooks instead of the session-scoped versions developed in op3/320/gd5. Three defects:

title: CLI: restructure install into all / basic / project subcommands
description:
## Context
The current xtrm install flow is monolithic and interactive. Users need granular control: some want everything, some only skills, some only project hooks. The new structure gives three clear install profiles while preserving all existing functionality.

## New install command structure

### xtrm install all
Global install of everything:
- All skills (skills/)
- All global hooks: general hooks (Python + main-guard.mjs) + beads gate hooks (beads-*.mjs)
- MCP server registration (~/.claude/mcp.json or settings.json)
- Runs beads+dolt dependency check BEFORE installing beads hooks (see separate issue)
- Prompts: 'Install beads+dolt? Required for workflow enforcement hooks [y/N]'
- On yes or already installed: installs beads hooks
- On no: installs everything except beads hooks, prints note

### xtrm install basic
Global install without beads hooks:
- All skills (skills/)
- General global hooks only (Python hooks + main-guard.mjs)
- MCP server registration
- No beads dependency check — beads hooks are intentionally excluded
- Safe to run anywhere with zero external deps

### xtrm install project
Project-scoped install (must be run from a project directory with a .git root):
- Installs project-skills/ (py-quality-gate, ts-quality-gate, tdd-guard, service-skills-set)
- Merges settings.json hooks into project's .claude/settings.json (deep merge, no clobber)
- Fails clearly if not in a git project directory

## Flags (all subcommands)
- --dry-run: show what would be installed, no writes
- --yes / -y: non-interactive, accept all prompts
- --no-mcp: skip MCP registration
- --force: overwrite existing files

## Acceptance criteria
- xtrm install all installs all components, runs beads check
- xtrm install basic installs skills + general hooks + mcp, skips beads
- xtrm install project installs project-skills into cwd
- --dry-run prints install manifest without writing
- --yes suppresses all prompts (for CI/automation)
- Banner displayed in all subcommand views
- No regression: existing install behavior preserved or improved

## Open idea review
Should there be even finer granularity? e.g., xtrm install hooks, xtrm install skills, xtrm install mcp as atomic subcommands composable by power users? The three profiles (all/basic/project) cover 90% of use cases but flags like --no-mcp within each profile may be cleaner than extra subcommands. What is the right granularity boundary?
close_reason: Implemented xtrm install all/basic subcommands on feat/install-subcommands

title: Remove main-guard from project-skills/
description:
## Context
main-guard is a global/user-scope hook. Having it in project-skills/ creates a false impression that it is project-scoped, and the CJS source there is now superseded by the canonical hooks/main-guard.mjs (after migration in ojt).

## What to do
- Delete project-skills/main-guard/ directory entirely
- Remove any references to it in CLI installer logic, README.md, and CLAUDE.md
- Verify no test or doc links point to project-skills/main-guard/

## Acceptance criteria
- project-skills/ contains only: py-quality-gate, ts-quality-gate, tdd-guard, service-skills-set
- No broken references in CLI or docs
- main-guard is only documented as a global hook in hooks/README.md

## Open idea review
Should there be a lightweight project-skill wrapper that configures the global main-guard per-project (e.g. custom protected branches list)? Or is the MAIN_GUARD_PROTECTED_BRANCHES env var sufficient? Consider whether project-level override is a future need.
close_reason: Deleted project-skills/main-guard/, updated tests, docs, and help.ts. All 34 tests pass.

title: Migrate specialists-main-guard.mjs into hooks/ as canonical main-guard.mjs
description:
## Context
The installed ~/.claude/hooks/specialists-main-guard.mjs is the superior version of main-guard: it covers Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit/Bash and blocks git commit + git push on protected branches. The project-skills/main-guard source is an older CJS version with narrower coverage. Since main-guard is a global/user-scope hook, it belongs in xtrm-tools hooks/ — not in project-skills.

## What to do
1. Copy ~/.claude/hooks/specialists-main-guard.mjs → hooks/main-guard.mjs
2. Update header: 'Installed by: xtrm install' (not specialists install)
3. Rename consistently — hooks/main-guard.mjs becomes the canonical file name

## The installed version capabilities (preserve all of these)
- Protects main/master branches
- Blocks: Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit (structured tools)
- Blocks: git commit, git push via Bash
- Falls through cleanly for all other Bash commands
- Falls back gracefully when not in a git repo
- Uses MAIN_GUARD_PROTECTED_BRANCHES env var for test override

## Acceptance criteria
- hooks/main-guard.mjs exists with full content of current specialists-main-guard.mjs
- Header updated, no functional changes

## Note
Issue xyi (main-guard Bash write bypass) and the reviewer's default-deny Bash recommendation are tracked separately — this is a pure migration issue, not a fix pass. Fix comes after migration.
close_reason: Migrated to hooks/ in PR #17, merged to main

title: Migrate beads gate hooks into hooks/ as global source of truth
description:
## Context
xtrm-tools is global/user-scope. All work done on beads hooks this session happened in ~/.claude/hooks/ (installed by specialists) but specialists is project-scope only. These files belong in xtrm-tools hooks/ and must be installed by the xtrm CLI at user scope (~/.claude/).

## Files to migrate
Copy from ~/.claude/hooks/ into hooks/:
- beads-edit-gate.mjs (session-scoped edit gate via bd kv claim)
- beads-stop-gate.mjs (session-scoped stop gate via bd kv claim)
- beads-commit-gate.mjs (session-scoped commit gate via bd kv claim)
- beads-close-memory-prompt.mjs (kv claim cleanup + post-close reminder)
- beads-gate-utils.mjs (shared primitives: resolveCwd, isBeadsProject, getSessionClaim, getTotalWork, getInProgress, clearSessionClaim, withSafeBdContext)

## Changes needed on migration
- Update header comments: 'Installed by: xtrm install' (not specialists)
- Verify relative import in hooks resolves correctly after location change: import from './beads-gate-utils.mjs' — static ES module imports resolve relative to file location, not CWD, so this should work as-is
- Ensure hooks/ README.md documents these new JS hooks alongside existing Python hooks

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 files present in hooks/ with correct content matching current ~/.claude/hooks/ versions
- README.md in hooks/ updated to list and describe each hook
- No functional changes — only relocation and comment updates

## Open idea review
Should hooks/ be split into hooks/python/ and hooks/js/ subdirectories for clarity as the JS hooks grow? Currently hooks/ is flat Python-only. Consider whether the flat structure still makes sense with mixed languages.
close_reason: Migrated to hooks/ in PR #17, merged to main

Allow: git pull, git fetch, git log/diff/status (read-only), git checkout/switch, git worktree, gh pr commands. Implementation: read stdin JSON, extract tool_input.command, get current branch via git branch --show-current, pattern match against blocked operations. title: audit(worktree): decide what gets gitignore'd or skip-worktree'd on every new worktree
description:
Operator question raised 2026-05-12: 'Probably would need a pass to decide what to gitignore when creating worktrees if that's possible in the whole repo.'

Today's xtrm-cbjo / unitAI-yvqmf fix handles .beads/ specifically via skip-worktree. The same shape problem exists for other paths that appear in worktrees but should never be committed back to main from a chain branch. Candidates uncovered in prior sessions:

- .beads/* — addressed by xtrm-cbjo (skip-worktree + rm)
- .pi/npm/ — currently symlinked to parent's .pi/npm/ on worktree create (cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts symlinkPiNpmCache, plus specialists' symlinkPiNpmCache). Worth asserting it stays clean of accidental commits.
- .pi/extensions/ — global symlink resolution; presumably clean today but verify.
- .specialists/default/ + .specialists/user/ — symlinked into worktree by ensureWorktreeSpecialists. Symlinks are themselves git-tracked candidates; verify they cannot be staged into a chain branch.
- .claude/skills — symlink to ../.xtrm/skills/active. Similar risk.
- .claude/settings.local.json — written per-worktree, gitignored already (verify).
- .xtrm/session-meta.json — gitignored already (verify).
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md — gitnexus stats counter scrub (xtrm-c6sf) addresses one specific drift source; broader audit may surface others.
- Per-tool runtime caches (specialists' observability.db, jobs/, ready/) — out of scope for worktree-tracked files but worth confirming layout.

What this bead should produce:
1. A canonical list of 'worktree-internal artifacts' grouped by mechanism (gitignored at parent level / skip-worktree'd at worktree create / symlinked from parent / written per-worktree only).
2. For each, the documented contract — e.g. '.beads/* is skip-worktree'd at worktree create; never stage .beads from a worktree' — added to update-xt SKILL.md and/or a new worktree-hygiene SKILL.
3. A defense layer: a pre-commit hook or similar that refuses to stage from the worktree if the path is on the prohibited list. This duplicates xt end's pre-push guard but at commit time, before bad commits land on the branch.
4. Verify there is no current way for a developer or specialist's git add -A to stage one of these paths into a chain branch.

Discovered-from: xtrm-cbjo and operator follow-up question. Pairs with infra repo bug (PR #39 / #40 incident) which exposed the same class of hazard for .beads specifically.

title: fix(repo-discovery): skip .worktrees and worktrees subdirs to avoid over-reporting transient worktree paths
description: PROBLEM: scanXtrmRepos descends into .worktrees/ and .xtrm/worktrees/ — every transient specialist / xt-claude worktree that inherits a .xtrm/ folder from its branch gets surfaced as a 'managed' or 'incomplete' repo. On a real sweep this means 11+ noise rows. They're not actual repos to update. SUCCESS: scanXtrmRepos skips directories named '.worktrees' (specialists style) and 'worktrees' (xt-claude style — appears under .xtrm/). Sweep output drops the transient noise. SCOPE: cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts (extend SKIP_DIRS), cli/src/tests/repo-discovery.test.ts (regression test), cli/dist rebuild. NON_GOALS: do not change the .git / node_modules skip behavior; do not add a generic path-pattern filter. CONSTRAINTS: directory-name-based skip only (cheap); accept very-rare false positive if a project legitimately names a top-level dir 'worktrees'. VALIDATION: new test asserts .worktrees/foo with a .xtrm/ inside is NOT discovered; same for .xtrm/worktrees/foo. OUTPUT: 1-line SKIP_DIRS change + test.
close_reason: Added .worktrees and worktrees to SKIP_DIRS in scanXtrmRepos. Sweep output no longer surfaces transient specialist/xt-claude worktree paths as managed or incomplete repos. 2 new regression tests; 7/7 pass.

title: Scrub gitnexus stats counter from tracked CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md + add regression check
description: PROBLEM: tracked CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md carry stale 'X symbols, Y relationships, Z execution flows' counter content from before specialists commit fd60db04 (2026-05-09) added --skip-agents-md --no-stats. Counter values dirty git state every time a tool runs 'gitnexus analyze' without those flags (manual operator runs, future tools that forget). Make the release cleaner by (a) scrubbing the existing counter from both files, (b) adding a check:* gate that fails build if the pattern reappears. SUCCESS: (a) CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md gitnexus:start blocks no longer contain stats counter, (b) new scripts/check-gitnexus-no-counter.mjs scans tracked CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md for the 'NNN symbols, NNN relationships' regex and exits 1 if found, (c) check wired into package.json scripts. SCOPE: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, scripts/check-gitnexus-no-counter.mjs (new), package.json. NON_GOALS: do not rewrite the rest of the gitnexus block; do not modify xt template; do not change gitnexus package itself. VALIDATION: npm run check:gitnexus-no-counter passes; manually verify CLAUDE.md grep for 'symbols, .* relationships' returns no matches. OUTPUT: scrubbed files + new check + wired in package.json.
close_reason: Scrubbed stale stats counter from CLAUDE.md (lines 362 + 596) and AGENTS.md (line 261). Added scripts/check-gitnexus-no-counter.mjs to detect regressions; wired into package.json prepublishOnly. Verified: npm run check:gitnexus-no-counter passes.

title: xt update --root: discover all .xtrm repos and surface registry-missing as warning
description: PROBLEM: cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts:findManagedRepos only discovers repos with both .xtrm/ AND .xtrm/registry.json. Repos with a .xtrm/ directory but no registry.json (partially initialized, broken install, mid-migration) are silently skipped during xt update --root sweeps. Operators may have stale incomplete installs they don't realize need attention. SUCCESS: (a) findManagedRepos returns both managed (has registry) and incomplete (.xtrm/ exists but no registry.json) lists; (b) xt update --root surfaces incomplete repos in the output with a distinct status row (e.g., 'incomplete' or 'needs-init'); (c) --apply only proceeds with managed repos — incomplete ones are reported with a remediation hint ('run xt init or xt install'); (d) --json output includes both groups. SCOPE: cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts (expose incomplete repos), cli/src/commands/update.ts (surface in output + status), cli/src/tests/repo-discovery.test.ts or equivalent (regression test), cli/dist/* rebuild. NON_GOALS: do not auto-fix incomplete repos (would be destructive); do not change xt install's discovery behavior (it scans cwd, not roots); do not modify the registry format. CONSTRAINTS: preserve existing managed-repo flow; backward-compatible JSON shape (add 'incomplete' as a new key/field, don't rename existing). VALIDATION: targeted vitest on the new discovery + update tests; manual smoke: 'mkdir /tmp/xt-test/{a,b}/.xtrm && touch /tmp/xt-test/a/.xtrm/registry.json && xt update --root /tmp/xt-test' shows a as managed and b as incomplete. OUTPUT: changed files, test evidence.
close_reason: Added scanXtrmRepos to cli/src/core/repo-discovery.ts (splits into managed + incomplete). xt update --root now surfaces incomplete repos (.xtrm/ present but no registry.json) with status 'incomplete' and a remediation hint ('run xt init or xt install'). Never auto-fixed — non-destructive surfacing only. findManagedRepos kept as backward-compatible thin wrapper. 5 new tests in cli/src/tests/repo-discovery.test.ts; full suite 517/0/98.

title: fix(cli): worktree provisioning should exclude .beads/ from per-worktree git tracking
description: PROBLEM: cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts:208-213 replaces the tracked .beads/ directory with a symlink to the parent repo's .beads/ (per commit 63d2bb1 — bd hooks inside worktrees must route to the parent's dolt server). Side effect: every checkpoint commit by an edit-capable specialist (executor, debugger) captures the directory→symlink swap as 1.7k lines of phantom .beads/ deletions + a new symlink file, polluting the chain diff. Reviewers FAIL on out-of-scope deletions; orchestrator must manually rewrite every checkpoint commit. Hit 5/5 chains during xtrm-4amc epic 2026-05-11. SUCCESS: edit-capable specialist dispatched into a fresh worktree produces a checkpoint commit with ZERO .beads/* paths (no phantom deletions, no symlink-add), without breaking bd hook routing. Operator xt claude worktrees also clean. SCOPE: cli/src/utils/worktree-session.ts (the .beads symlink block at lines 208-213), cli/dist/* rebuild, a new regression test under cli/test/ or cli/src/tests/. NON_GOALS: do NOT change the symlink itself (bd hook routing depends on it); do not touch parent .beads/ at all; no broader worktree-provisioning rewrite. CONSTRAINTS: preserve EXACT current symlink semantics (rm + symlinkSync to parent's .beads); the fix must be ADDITIVE — extra calls after the symlinkSync. Implementation plan (executor must verify this is correct): (a) get per-worktree gitdir via 'git -C <worktree> rev-parse --absolute-git-dir', (b) append '.beads' to <gitdir>/info/exclude to suppress the new symlink as untracked-noise, (c) run 'git -C <worktree> ls-files -- .beads' to get tracked .beads/* files, then 'git update-index --skip-worktree <files>' so the directory-to-symlink swap doesn't show as deletions for those tracked files. Both operations must be wrapped in try/catch — non-fatal if they fail (existing block already does this). CRITICAL — worktree hygiene per memory specialist-worktree-provisioning-in-xtrm-tools-replaces-trac: when YOU commit your fix, do NOT include any .beads/* paths. Use 'git add' with explicit source/test paths. After bd close auto-export, run 'git restore --staged .beads/issues.jsonl' before final commit. VALIDATION: (1) targeted unit test that mocks/spies the post-symlink git update-index call, (2) end-to-end: 'cd cli && npx vitest run <new-test>' shows the new test passes, (3) full 'npm test --workspace cli' shows 0 fails (should match current 508 passed baseline), (4) manual smoke (executor should run this): create a tmp worktree via 'git worktree add /tmp/test-wt feature/test', simulate the symlink swap by running the new code path, then 'git -C /tmp/test-wt status' must NOT show any .beads/* changes. title: Friction: Dolt server crashed mid-session, journal corrupted at offset 73333475
description:
PROBLEM: During 2026-05-09 orchestrator session at ~02:48Z, the shared-server Dolt instance became unreachable, then refused restart with 'possible data loss detected in journal file at offset 73333475: corrupted journal'. Recovery required either dolt fsck --revive-journal-with-data-loss (with data-loss warning) or operator manual intervention. Operator restart of bd dolt start eventually recovered.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS: concurrent access from main repo + multiple worktrees (each spawning per-worktree Dolt connections) overwhelmed the shared-server, OR a specific bd write triggered the journal corruption. Specialist worktrees were running 4-6 parallel chains writing to bd at peak.

OBSERVED IMPACT: ~30 minutes of orchestrator forced into 'in-flight specialists keep running but no new beads' mode until operator restart. Lost some bd state (planner-created sub-beads xtrm-9xg2.1 visible in main but lqjn closure may have been on planner branch).

PROPOSED FIXES:
- bd doctor should warn early when Dolt journal size approaches risk thresholds
- shared-server should serialize writes more aggressively under concurrent worktree load
- worktree bd config should default to remote (read-only or write-via-API) instead of direct Dolt connection

OWNER: beads upstream (Dolt safety) + xtrm-tools (worktree config policy).

title: Friction: epic guard refuses dispatch on sub-bead with unmerged sibling chains
description:
PROBLEM: 'sp run <role> --bead <epic-child>' refuses dispatch with 'Refusing worktree dispatch for bead 'xtrm-9xg2.2': epic 'xtrm-9xg2' has unmerged sibling chains with substantive commits.' This forces --force-stale-base for every dispatch on epic children. Even with --force-stale-base, subsequent reviewer/sanity dispatches on the SAME chain may fail silently (no error message, no sp ps entry).

OBSERVED: 2026-05-09 — xtrm-9xg2.2 + xtrm-9xg2.3 each required --force-stale-base on initial executor dispatch; subsequent reviewer/sanity could not be dispatched at all (silently dropped).

WORKAROUND: skip reviewer/sanity for epic children; defer to operator.

PROPOSED FIXES:
- chain dispatches WITHIN the same chain (same root bead) should not be epic-blocked
- --force-stale-base should be inherited by child dispatches under same chain root
- silent dispatch failures should at minimum print a refusal reason

OWNER: jaggerxtrm/specialists repo.

title: Friction: chain branches polluted by AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md gitnexus stats refresh + .pi/npm + pnpm artifacts
description:
PROBLEM: Every executor/debugger turn produces a checkpoint commit. A pre/post-commit hook also re-touches AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md to refresh GitNexus 'symbols/relationships/flows' stat counts, AND .pi/npm gets accidentally staged on most chains. cli/pnpm-lock.yaml and cli/pnpm-workspace.yaml occasionally land too when pnpm is invoked inside worktree. Result: every chain branch carries 4-7 noise commits unrelated to the bead's actual scope, polluting reviewer diffs and merge history.

OBSERVED: 2026-05-09 — ALL 18 chain branches preserved in this session contain AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md noise; many contain .pi/npm; u3t branch contains cli/pnpm-lock.yaml + cli/pnpm-workspace.yaml (debugger had to manually scrub).

PROPOSED FIXES:
1. Idempotent AGENTS/CLAUDE generation (already tracked under xtrm-i4uu/xtrm-9xg2.* — in progress)
2. Add .pi/npm and cli/pnpm-* to a default .gitignore at xt install time
3. Auto-checkpoint commits should be amended/squashed at chain end before reviewer (per-turn 'turn N' commits aren't useful for reviewer; cumulative diff is)
4. Specialist script should refuse to stage outside the bead's declared SCOPE

OWNER: xtrm-tools (.gitignore + checkpoint hygiene) + specialists (auto-checkpoint commit strategy).

title: Friction: bd close fails inside worktree (Dolt DB not found)
description:
PROBLEM: Specialists running inside worktrees consistently fail bd commands with 'database jaggers_agent_tools not found on Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:<port>'. This blocks bd close, bd remember, bd update --notes from inside worktree, forcing orchestrator to close beads from main repo.

OBSERVED: 2026-05-09 session — every executor/debugger reported '[bd] Could not run reliable bd updates/close due Dolt/beads DB error in environment'. Each worktree spawns its own Dolt port (40207 etc), separate from main repo's port (3308).

ROOT CAUSE: Dolt shared-server config (dolt.shared-server: true in .beads/config.yaml) routes main repo to ~/.beads/shared-server/, but worktrees may not inherit this config OR may try to spawn their own per-worktree server.

WORKAROUND: orchestrator owns bead lifecycle; specialists never close beads.

NOTE: xtrm-19e5 (closed PASS this session) addresses scaffolding shared-server config in xt update — but doesn't fix the worktree case.

PROPOSED FIX:
- worktree should inherit parent repo's bd config (especially dolt.shared-server flag)
- OR worktree should explicitly route bd commands to parent repo's Dolt instance
- specialist scripts could detect worktree and use 'bd --remote' style flag

OWNER: xtrm-tools (worktree provisioning) or specialists (specialist runtime).
notes: Verification 2026-05-12: bd read/write/kv/remember all succeed from worktree (xtrm-tools-xt-claude-r3ws). Original Dolt port-conflict error not reproduced.
close_reason: Verified 2026-05-12: bd ops (read, kv, remember, update --notes) all succeed from inside xtrm worktree. Shared Dolt server resolves correctly. Specialist-runtime verification deferred — primitive works.

title: Canonicalize hook and active-view terminology across docs and specialist assets
description:
## Context

Audit found canonical layout drift across docs and specialist-facing assets. Runtime code and package metadata treat hooks as `.xtrm/hooks/` and skills active view as flat `.xtrm/skills/active`, but source still carries stale terminology like `.xtrm/hooks/default/` and `.xtrm/skills/active/{claude,pi}`.

Known stale references inside current worktree:
- `docs/xtrm-directory.md`
- `docs/skills.md`
- `docs/skills-tier-architecture.md`
- `.xtrm/skills/default/using-specialists-v2/SKILL.md`
- `.xtrm/skills/default/update-specialists/SKILL.md`
- `.specialists/user/parallel-review.specialist.json`

## What to build

Document and apply single canonical contract for hook path and active skills layout across human-facing source assets.

- Decide and record whether hook default tier exists.
- Replace stale `.xtrm/hooks/default/` references with canonical hook path.
- Replace stale per-runtime active view references with flat `.xtrm/skills/active` path.
- Update any specialist config/examples that still point at `active/pi` or `active/claude`, or mark them explicitly historical/transient when they must stay.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Canonical decision recorded: managed hooks live under `.xtrm/hooks/`; no live `.xtrm/hooks/default/` tier remains in docs/skills/beads guidance.
- [ ] Human-facing docs and specialist assets in source no longer describe `.xtrm/skills/active/{claude,pi}` as current runtime layout.
- [ ] Any intentionally historical references are labeled deprecated/transient instead of looking canonical.
- [ ] Acceptance language for downstream work points at real hook and active paths.

## Approach notes

Use current runtime code as source of truth:
- `cli/src/core/skills-runtime-views.ts`
- `cli/src/core/pi-runtime.ts`
- `scripts/gen-registry.mjs`
- `package.json`

title: Security audit: xxf4 pack-aware scaffolder
description:
PROBLEM: Pack scaffolder writes to canonical pack path and creates legacy symlink. Touches file system path resolution, env var (XTRM_PACK), symlink creation. Risk surface: path traversal via XTRM_PACK, symlink-following attacks on legacy paths.
SUCCESS: Findings isolate real risk (path traversal in XTRM_PACK, symlink overwrite, untrusted-input write) or confirm no obvious issue.
SCOPE: Same diff as xxf4 sanity. Use cumulative-diff command.
NON_GOALS: No edits.
CONSTRAINTS: LOW permissions, scan-only.
OUTPUT: Recommendations or 'no findings'.
notes:
Audit progress: inspected pack-aware scaffolder/bootstrap path handling. Confirmed reachable untrusted-path writes via XTRM_PACK and service_id traversal; confirmed destructive legacy symlink replacement path via ensure_legacy_symlink + unsanitized service_id. No edits made.
### Specialist Output — security-auditor (job 52681d) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Scoped audit found real filesystem risk in pack-aware scaffolder. Main issue: untrusted `XTRM_PACK` and `service_id` flow into path construction without containment checks. Legacy symlink helper can also delete attacker-selected directory before replacing with symlink. No dependency CVEs reviewed. No edits made.

## Status
findings

## Changes
- No file changes.
- Reviewed:
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py`
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py`
  - `.xtrm/skills/default/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py`

## Verification
- `bd show xtrm-0oxc`
- `git diff e35fa46..5bac81c -- skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py .xtrm/skills/default/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py .xtrm/skills/default/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py`
- `rg -n "XTRM_PACK|get_pack_path|ensure_legacy_symlink|scaffold_service_skill" skills .xtrm -g '*/creating-service-skills/scripts/*.py'`
- GitNexus:
  - `gitnexus_query(...)`
  - `gitnexus_context(name="scaffold_service_skill")`
  - `gitnexus_context(name="get_registry_path")`
  - `gitnexus_context(name="register_service")`
  - `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope="all")`
- Safe path-resolution demo:
  - `python3` snippet showing `../../` and absolute paths escape intended roots

## Risks
- High: arbitrary repo/outside-repo writes via `XTRM_PACK`
- High: arbitrary path traversal via unsanitized `service_id`
- High: destructive delete/replace in `ensure_legacy_symlink` when `service_id` traverses path
- Medium: registry write path confusion when canonical pack registry pre-exists outside intended tree

## Follow-ups
- Reject absolute `XTRM_PACK` unless under `<project>/.xtrm/skills/user/packs`
- Canonicalize `XTRM_PACK` with `resolve()` and enforce parent containment
- Validate `service_id` against strict allowlist like `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$`
- Before mkdir/write/delete/symlink, resolve target and assert under intended base dir
- Replace delete-then-symlink logic with safer guarded creation flow; never `rmtree` path derived from untrusted input
- Add tests for `XTRM_PACK=../../...`, absolute pack path, `service_id=../../...`, existing legacy dir/symlink edge cases

## Security Audit Summary
- Scope reviewed:
  - Pack-aware scaffolder diff for bootstrap/scaffolder path resolution and legacy symlink creation
- Overall risk: high
- Mode(s) used:
  - static secure-code review
  - agent/config security scan
  - bounty-style exploitability triage
- Commands/sources used:
  - local source paths above
  - GitNexus context/detect_changes
  - safe local path normalization demo

## Findings
- ID: SEC-XXF4-001
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-22 Path Traversal, CWE-73 External Control of File Name or Path
- Evidence:
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py:62-69`
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py:68-87`
  - mirrored same logic in `.xtrm/skills/default/...`
  - `get_pack_path()` returns `Path(env_pack)` directly if absolute, else `packs_root / env_pack`, with no `resolve()`/containment check
  - `scaffold_service_skill()` writes directories/files under `pack_path / service_id`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Reachable from local CLI/runtime env. `XTRM_PACK` fully controls write base. Relative values with `../` escape pack root. Absolute values escape repo entirely.
- Impact:
  - Arbitrary file/directory creation under attacker-selected path. If target path exists or later registry file exists there, can also alter registry location behavior.
- Recommended fix:
  - Resolve path, require result under canonical packs root, reject absolute external paths and traversal segments.
- Verification after fix:
  - Unit tests proving `XTRM_PACK=../../tmp`, `XTRM_PACK=/tmp/p`, `XTRM_PACK=packA` behavior; only packA under canonical root allowed.

- ID: SEC-XXF4-002
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-22 Path Traversal, CWE-20 Improper Input Validation
- Evidence:
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py:73-87`
  - `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/scaffolder.py:418-434`
  - `service_id` comes from `sys.argv[2]` or compose keys, then used in `skill_dir = pack_path / service_id` and `legacy_dir = Path(project_root) / ".claude" / "skills" / service_id`
  - no validation found for `service_id`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Direct CLI arg route. No sanitization. `../../victim` or deeper escapes intended root.
- Impact:
  - Arbitrary path write primitive for generated files and directories. Can target repo files or outside-repo paths depending on current permissions.
- Recommended fix:
  - Enforce strict service-id pattern and resolved-path containment for both canonical and legacy targets.
- Verification after fix:
  - Tests for rejected values: `../x`, `a/../b`, absolute path, empty, dot segments.

### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job 7b33f0) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `cli/src/core/beads-shared-server.ts`, `cli/src/commands/install.ts`, `cli/src/commands/update.ts`, `cli/src/commands/doctor.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `cli/src/commands/update.ts:34-47`
  - Issue: `hasChanges` treats `!sharedServerConfigured` as drift even when repo has no `.beads/` setup.
  - Why it matters: `xt update --apply` will keep reporting / re-running “refresh” for repos not migrated to beads, so non-beads repos never reach stable `already-current` state.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Gate shared-server drift check on `.beads/` presence, or make `isSharedBeadsServerConfigured()` return a tri-state so absent beads is skipped, not counted as drift.

- Location: `cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:241-243, 267-273, 311-318`
  - Issue: doctor always counts missing shared-server config as error, even when `.beads/` is absent.
  - Why it matters: clean repos without beads now fail doctor and print fix advice that assumes migration already exists; violates “clean handling when `.beads/` absent.”
  - Suggested executor instruction: Treat absent `.beads/` as “not applicable” for Cat B shared-server check, and only surface error when `.beads/config.yaml` exists but is wrong.

## Non-findings
- Not flagging `buildConfigContent()` or process-stop logic as smells; they look simple enough for this pass.
- Not reviewing product intent of whether beads should be mandatory; that belongs to reviewer / requirements gate.

## Handoff
Need executor follow-up on absence-aware shared-server detection. After fix, re-run bounded diff check and doctor/update flows.

### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job ba2fed) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs`, `scripts/gen-registry.mjs`, `scripts/verify-specialists-vendor.mjs`, plus `package.json` integration
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs:117-122` / `.xtrm/specialists-source.json`
  - Issue: `generatedAt` bakes current timestamp into source marker.
  - Why it matters: same input produces different marker/registry bytes every run, so release artifact not deterministic; noisy diffs, cache misses, impossible reproducible rebuilds.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Drop `generatedAt` from published marker, or move it to non-published build log only.

- Location: `scripts/gen-registry.mjs:90-96`
  - Issue: manifest parse failures collapse to `null` instead of hard fail.
  - Why it matters: broken or malformed `specialists-source.json` still lets registry generation continue, writing `metadata.specialists: null` and hiding root cause until later.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Fail fast on missing/invalid specialists manifest; only accept validated JSON schema.

- Location: `scripts/vendor-specialists-skills.mjs:120` + `scripts/verify-specialists-vendor.mjs:31-33`
  - Issue: `source` field stores local checkout path in git-sha mode, and verifier only checks equality with registry copy.
  - Why it matters: published manifest becomes machine-specific and non-portable; verification can still pass while artifact leaks env-specific absolute path.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Replace local path with stable canonical source identifier, and validate allowed `source` shape in verifier.

## Non-findings
- Not flagging `--specialists-ref/--specialists-package` arg plumbing; rough but not broken.
- Not flagging asset copy logic; looks linear and simple.
- Not judging release-policy choice for where verification runs; reviewer/test-runner territory.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer with 3 concrete issues. Fix target: manifest determinism, hard-fail validation, stable source identity.

description:

notes:
### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job 19279a) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py`, `skills/updating-service-skills/scripts/drift_detector.py`, mirrored `.xtrm/skills/default/...` copies, diff from HEAD
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `skills/updating-service-skills/scripts/drift_detector.py:224-228`
  - Issue: `scan` now hard-fails when `get_registry_path()` points at missing registry, before `scan_drift()` can do its old graceful empty-result path.
  - Why it matters: breaks advisory scan on fresh repos / no-registry state; also duplicates registry resolution and can turn a non-error into exit 1.
  - Suggested executor instruction: remove precheck from `main()` and let `scan_drift()` own missing-registry handling, or keep precheck but return `No drift detected` instead of hard exit.

- Location: `skills/creating-service-skills/scripts/bootstrap.py:93-102`
  - Issue: `_select_pack_registry()` sorts `Path` objects and picks first lexicographic path when multiple pack registries exist.
  - Why it matters: lexicographic path order is arbitrary, so fallback can silently choose wrong pack if more than one exists; warning says “using” one path but no rule ties it to canonical pack.
  - Suggested executor instruction: replace path sort fallback with explicit selection rule (single-pack only, pack manifest priority, or deterministic metadata key); if multiple packs valid, return error instead of guessing.

## Non-findings
- Did not flag `Path.glob("*/service-registry.json")`: safe on missing dir, lazy enough for this scale.
- Did not flag mirror duplication: mechanical sync, not smell by itself.
- Did not flag broad `except Exception` in `update_sync_time()`: ugly, but existing pattern; reviewer owns whether to tighten.

## Handoff
Proceed to reviewer with 2 concrete smells. Main risk: scan path behavior change and pack fallback ambiguity. No code edits done.

title: Code-sanity scan: CI registry-pack parity gate (xtrm-02er)
description:
PROBLEM: Executor added scripts/check-registry-pack-parity.mjs and CI workflow steps for registry-to-npm-pack parity. Need a smell pass before reviewer.
SUCCESS: Findings flag concrete simplification, allowlist clarity, error-handling gap, or path-resolution issues; or confirm clean shape with caveats.
SCOPE: Executor diff in scripts/check-registry-pack-parity.mjs, package.json, .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/publish.yml (job a7a8d3, branch feature/xtrm-02er-executor). Look at HEAD diff (executor auto-checkpoints).
NON_GOALS: No edits, no scope expansion, no reviewer gate decision.
CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY, cite exact lines/symbols, evaluate parity script logic for false-positive/negative risk.
VALIDATION: Findings reference actual diff lines.
OUTPUT: FINDINGS with severity, or OK with caveats.
notes:
### Specialist Output — code-sanity (job bf6dcc) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `scripts/check-registry-pack-parity.mjs`, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/publish.yml` at HEAD diff
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `scripts/check-registry-pack-parity.mjs:54-61`
  - Issue: `getPackFiles()` reads only `packResults[0]?.files`.
  - Why it matters: repo has workspaces (`package.json` `workspaces: ["cli","packages/pi-extensions"]`), so `npm pack --dry-run --json` can emit more than one result if packaging scope changes. This code silently drops later entries, giving false-negative parity passes.
  - Suggested executor instruction: flatten all `packResults[*].files` or fail if more than one pack result appears, so parity covers every packed artifact.

## Non-findings
- Ignored CI placement/order and extra workflow duplication; belongs reviewer scope, not smell pass.
- Ignored whether allowlist entries are policy-correct; only looked for concrete implementation brittleness.

## Handoff
One real smell: pack parsing too narrow. Fix executor, rerun parity logic, then send back to reviewer.

## Machine-readable block

description:
PROBLEM: Debugger fixed isSourceRepoRoot to accept packaged wrapper markers (.xtrm/registry.json + .xtrm/skills) and removed silent fallback in runPreflight. findRepoRoot has 7 direct callers (CRITICAL blast radius). Need a smell pass before reviewer.

SCOPE: Debugger diff in cli/src/utils/repo-root.ts, cli/src/commands/init.ts, cli/test/init-phases.test.ts, cli/test/repo-root.wrapper-layout.test.ts (job 5a7be1, branch feature/xtrm-tools-u3t-debugger).
NON_GOALS: No edits, no scope expansion, no reviewer gate decision.

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `cli/src/utils/repo-root.ts`, `cli/src/commands/init.ts`, `cli/test/init-phases.test.ts`, `cli/test/repo-root.wrapper-layout.test.ts`
- Confidence: high

## Findings
- Location: `cli/src/utils/repo-root.ts:21-30,42-50`
  - Issue: `findRepoRoot()` now accepts any ancestor with `.xtrm/registry.json + .xtrm/skills`, but first search path is `process.cwd()`. If CLI runs inside project that already has `.xtrm/` from install/init, function can return project root instead of wrapper/package root.
  - Why it matters: 7 direct callers treat return value as source/bundle root. `runPreflight()` uses it as `repoRoot` for `calculateDiff()`, so wrong root can make diff plan compare against project tree instead of packaged source. Same mismatch hits `docs`, `status`, `pi`, `claude`, `install-pi`, `clean`.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Make wrapper detection disambiguate bundle root from project root. Prefer explicit bundle anchor under runtime path, or skip `process.cwd()` match when cwd is current project workspace.

- Location: `cli/src/utils/repo-root.ts:53-55`
  - Issue: error text only mentions `.xtrm/skills`, but implementation now also requires `.xtrm/registry.json`.
  - Why it matters: stale failure message sends user to wrong artifact when packaged wrapper layout missing or partial.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Update thrown message to name both required markers and wrapper layout path.

## Non-findings
- `any` in `runPreflight()` is old shape, not introduced by this diff.
- New test coverage for packaged wrapper layout is good; gap is collision case, not missing happy-path test.

Proceed to reviewer with 1 real compatibility risk. If executor wants fix, need root-resolution disambiguation before merge.
